title: Extract rotation scripts into versioned ConfigMaps
review_scope: Post external-review improvement cycle (berendt feedback, 4 warnings + 2 info items)
summary: All external reviewer feedback correctly addressed: conn.close() added to both scripts (W-001), HTML TODO comments on pre-existing hardcoded names in docs (W-002), VolumeMounts assertions refactored from index-based to find-by-name switch pattern matching the existing Volumes assertion style (W-003), sync cross-reference comments added to duplicated Python PATCH blocks (W-004), set -e upgraded to set -eu in both scripts (I-002). No new issues introduced.

verification.lint: golangci-lint reports 1 pre-existing issue in reconcile_networkpolicy_test.go:540 (unchanged file, out of scope)
verification.shellcheck: shellcheck binary not available in CI environment — make shellcheck exits 127 (not a regression, tool not installed)

W-001.description: conn.close() added before success print in both fernet_rotate.sh:26 and credential_rotate.sh:27

W-002.description: HTML TODO comments added to 4 pre-existing hardcoded rows (keystone-db-sync, keystone-bootstrap, keystone-api Deployment, keystone-api Service) in docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md:1210-1213

W-003.description: VolumeMounts assertions in both test files refactored from index-based (container.VolumeMounts[3]) to find-by-name switch pattern, matching the existing Volumes assertion pattern in the same test

W-004.description: Sync cross-reference comments added to both scripts: '# NOTE: The Python K8s API PATCH block below MUST stay in sync with {sibling}.sh (CC-0073, W-004)'

I-001.description: E2E tests implicitly validate ConfigMap mounting through successful rotation — accepted as adequate

I-002.description: Both scripts changed from 'set -e' to 'set -eu' to detect unset variables

tests_checklist.item: Edge cases are covered (empty embed guard, HaveLen + find-by-name)
Test assertions are specific (exact mount paths, ReadOnly flags, ConfigMap prefixes, DefaultMode)
Find-by-name pattern consistent across both test files

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (switch-based find-by-name mirrors Volumes assertion)
Comments are accurate and reference correct CC/W IDs
Changes are minimal and scoped to feedback items only

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible
No scope creep — only feedback items addressed
W-002 correctly deferred as pre-existing with TODO tracking
W-004 correctly deferred with sync comments instead of premature extraction

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Existing duplication (Python PATCH block) acknowledged with sync comments, not over-engineered

fail_fast_checklist.item: set -eu catches unset variables before they reach Python

defensive_checklist.item: All external inputs validated (env vars via os.environ which raises KeyError)
HTTP response status checked before proceeding
